## [00:00 - 03:03]

Alright guys, so let's talk about rapid fire testing. Now this is a concept that we came up with inside of the agency about two years ago and specifically because we were working with coaches and consultants and these kinds of funnels. So what was happening was that we were building out all of these funnels with these email sequences, retargeting, tripwires, messaging, and the client was having to pull together these lead magnets, sometimes PDFs or video trainings, etc. etc. and it takes a lot of time to build those. It takes a lot of time and resources to build those. So sometimes you'd start working with a client and the funnel, you'd launch the funnel maybe you know three weeks in and after getting enough data and optimizing and changing things we would find that you know for two months on or something like that. The funnel that was working looked completely different. You know the offer was totally different or the lead magnet was totally different than what we initially started with. And we started to think okay surely there has to be an easier way to validate the concepts and the offers and the messaging before we go and put all this effort in. This is where we came up with rapid fire testing. Rapid fire testing is going to be using lead ads to validate these concepts and we're going to do three rounds inside of this framework. So the first round is where we go out with our lead ads and we test out different personas. So for example if you're selling, if your client is selling a program teaching people how to launch their own e-commerce stores, you're going to map out with your client's help hopefully all the different personas that make up that potential target audience. So is it existing e-commerce stores, owners, is it brand new ones, is it bizops, is it stay at home moms looking for who is it that they're targeting, right? And you're going to validate the audience, the persona by looking at which one is most responsive. So we're trying to build the funnel that's going to have the best traction right off the bat. Once we identify the persona we then go into the second phase of rapid fire testing again using lead ads here and with this one we're essentially testing out the hook or the offer. So here's where we test out different lead magnets without actually building the lead magnets. Now you're probably thinking to yourself well what happens when people opt in to the lead magnet. There is collateral damage when you run rapid fire testing and usually it's going to be about 20 to 50 leads, maybe even less than that. Don't worry about it though, what you do is once you actually build out the

## [03:03 - 04:49]

funnel you just email out those leads and you can even do that right away and say hey we're super sorry we just experienced an issue. We'll send it out to you as soon as we have it. So just be aware that that is the situation. However in all of our years of running this and working with people doing this type of testing we've had maybe one complaint and even then it was like oh where's my training? So I wouldn't even worry about this. Now once you've validated the offer and the hook we then activate the funnel so we build, we start to build the funnel and we start to activate it and then we go into our creative testing. So we're testing out different forms of copy, long form, short form, videos versus single image ads. Do we have a call to action button? Do we not? If we're using videos what is our thumbnail? So that's where you really test out the creative here and this one is an ongoing process this last round here. So this is rapid fire testing, this is the framework I'm going to be taking you through whether you're working with clients or you're doing this for yourself. I highly recommend that you follow this process. Is it the easiest solution? Absolutely not. You can already see that there's going to be quite a bit of work involved here but it is far less work than building out a funnel and discovering you know three weeks and after spending all this money that that funnel will not work. This is one way to protect results and also to save yourself long term, a lot of time and a lot of money. Alright so I'm going to take you through into the first round of setting up your rapid fire test.
